sourceMovie.title: The Angry Birds Movie
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Angry Birds Movie works as a animation movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The animation and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Moana
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Angry Birds Movie clicked for you, Moana (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and adventure, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Super Mario Bros. Movie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Angry Birds Movie clicked for you, The Super Mario Bros. Movie (2023) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and adventure, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Angry Birds Movie?
faq.answer: Try Moana, The Super Mario Bros. Movie, Ice Age: Dawn of the Dinosaurs—each shares DNA with The Angry Birds Movie while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Angry Birds Movie?
faq.answer: The Angry Birds Movie (2016) is best described as Animation, Adventure, Comedy, Family. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Angry Birds Movie part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Angry Birds Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
